Foam

Foundations Of Mathematics

Introduction

A member of a set is called an element and we write x∈Sx\in Sx∈S to mean that “x is an element of S”. With this in mind, we proceed to look at the real numbers

Real Numbers

The real numbers are an example of a mathematical object known as a field. For a set to be known as a field, It needs the + operation and the ⋅\cdot⋅ operation that satisfy the properties below. There are a total of 9 properties

Addition

  1. Addition must be associative
\[\alpha + (\beta + \gamma) = (\alpha + \beta) + \gamma\]
  1. Addition must be commutative
\[\alpha + \beta = \beta + \alpha\]
  1. An additive identity ,denoted as 000, must exist such that
\[\alpha + 0 = \alpha\]
  1. An additive inverse −α-\alpha−α must exist such that
\[\alpha + (-\alpha) = 0\]

Multiplication

  1. Multiplication must be commutative
\[\alpha * (\beta * \gamma) = (\alpha * \beta) * \gamma\]
  1. Multiplcation must be commutative
\[\alpha * \beta = \beta * \alpha\]
  1. An multiplicative identity which is denoted as 111 and is different from 000 must exist such that
\[\alpha * 1 = \alpha\]
  1. A multiplicative inverse must exist such that
\[\alpha * \alpha^{-1} = 1\]

Additional Properties

  1. The two must obey the distributive property such that
\[\alpha (\beta + \gamma) = (\alpha \cdot \beta) + (\alpha \cdot \gamma)\]
